question: Will 10 Fed rate cuts happen in 2026?
description:
This market will resolve according to the exact amount of cuts of 25 basis points in 2026 by the Fed (including any cuts made during the December meeting).

Emergency rate cuts outside of scheduled FOMC meetings will also count toward the total number of cuts in 2026. This market will remain open until December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, to account for any such emergency actions.

For example, if the Fed cuts rates by 50 bps after a meeting, it would be considered 2 cuts (of 25 bps each).

This market will resolve early to "No" if the specified number of cuts becomes impossible — i.e., if more cuts have already occurred than the strike in question.

Note that cuts between 1–24 bps (inclusive) will also be considered 1 rate cut.

The resolution source for this market will be FOMC statements after meetings scheduled in 2026 according to the official calendar: https://www.federalreserve.gov/monetarypolicy/fomccalendars.htm. The level and change of the target federal funds rate is also published at the official website of the Federal Reserve at https://www.federalreserve.gov/monetarypolicy/openmarket.htm.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader sentiment on Polymarket heavily favors minimal Fed rate cuts in 2026, with 37% implied probability for zero cuts (0 bps) and 26.5% for one (25 bps), aggregating to over 63% for at most one cut, driven by post-election repricing of inflationary pressures from anticipated Trump-era tariffs, tax cuts, and fiscal stimulus. This hawkish tilt contrasts the FOMC's December 2024 dot plot median of roughly two 25 bps cuts to 2.9% by year-end, as robust November jobs data (256K payrolls) and sticky core PCE inflation near 2.7% underscore economic resilience delaying deeper easing. Traders eye Q1 2025 CPI releases and January FOMC for pivotal shifts, with market-implied odds reflecting capital-backed caution on aggressive policy normalization.

question: Opensea FDV above $500M one day after launch?
description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if the Fully Diluted Valuation of Opensea's token is greater than the value specified in the title 1 day after launch. Otherwise, the market will resolve to "No."

The token must be actively, publicly transferable and tradable to be considered a launch.

"1 day after launch" is defined as 4:00 PM ET on the calendar day following launch. The resolution source for this market is the most liquid price source available. If Opensea doesn't launch a token by December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to "No".

question: Will Trump and Putin meet next in Belarus?
description:
This market will resolve according to the location of the next meeting between Donald Trump and Vladimir Putin between September 30 and June 30, 2026, 11:59 PM ET.

This market will resolve to "No meeting by June 30" if no qualifying meeting occurs during this market's timeframe.

A meeting is defined as any encounter where Putin and Trump are all present and interact with each other in person.

An exchange of words, handshake, direct conversation, or other clear personal interaction between the named individuals will qualify as a meeting. Merely standing in proximity, making eye contact, or being present in the same room or event without direct interaction will not qualify.

For this market, Gulf states are defined as the six members of the Gulf Cooperation Council (Bahrain, Kuwait, Oman, Qatar, Saudi Arabia, United Arab Emirates).

The primary resolution source for this market will be a consensus of credible reporting.

events.title: Where will Trump and Putin meet next?

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus overwhelmingly favors no Trump-Putin meeting by June 30 (84.5%), reflecting the absence of any official announcements or scheduled summits since Trump's January inauguration. Recent diplomatic exchanges, including post-election phone calls focused on Ukraine cease-fire talks, have not progressed to in-person venue planning amid U.S. sanctions on Russia and NATO allies' scrutiny. Logistical challenges, such as security protocols and host-country neutrality, further dampen near-term prospects. Low probabilities for specific locations like Gulf states (2.5%) or European venues stem from unverified rumors lacking primary confirmation, with traders pricing in geopolitical frictions and potential delays beyond summer as more likely.

question: Aziz Akhannouch out as Morocco Prime Minister by December 31, 2026?
description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if Aziz Akhannouch ceases to be Prime Minister of Morocco for any period of time between market creation and the specified date (ET). Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

An announcement of Aziz Akhannouch's resignation/removal before this market's end date will immediately resolve this market to "Yes", regardless of when the announced resignation/removal goes into effect.

If the specified individual is detained, effectively removed from the specified position, or otherwise permanently prevented from fulfilling the duties of the specified position within this market’s timeframe, it will qualify for a “Yes” resolution.

The resolution source for this market will be official information from Aziz Akhannouch and the government of Morocco; however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: King Mohammed VI's acceptance of Aziz Akhannouch's government resignation on September 10, 2024, relieved the longtime prime minister of his duties as head of government, propelling the 92% implied probability that Akhannouch will be out as Morocco's Prime Minister by December 31, 2026. The monarch tasked Akhannouch with proposing a new cabinet composition within days amid economic strains, youth unemployment, and inflation pressures fueling public protests. This rare royal intervention signals a major political shift, with traders betting heavily against Akhannouch's return to the premiership given the context of performance critiques and transition dynamics, though new government formation remains pending.

question: Opensea FDV above $5B one day after launch?
description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if the Fully Diluted Valuation of Opensea's token is greater than the value specified in the title 1 day after launch. Otherwise, the market will resolve to "No."

The token must be actively, publicly transferable and tradable to be considered a launch.

"1 day after launch" is defined as 4:00 PM ET on the calendar day following launch. The resolution source for this market is the most liquid price source available. If Opensea doesn't launch a token by December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to "No".

question: Mike Johnson out as Speaker by March 31?
description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if Mike Johnson announces that he will resign from his position as Speaker of the US House of Representatives, or otherwise ceases to be Speaker for any length of time, between April 9, and March 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

Note that an announcement of his resignation before the resolution date will suffice to resolve this market to "Yes" regardless of if/when he actually steps down.

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from the Government of the United States of America or official statements by Mike Johnson or his representative, however a consensus of credible reporting will suffice.

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from the Government of the United States of America or official statements by Mike Johnson or his representative, however a consensus of credible reporting will suffice.

eventMetadata.context_description: Republican House unity and President Trump's endorsement have solidified Mike Johnson's position as Speaker following his narrow re-election in January 2025 amid a slim 220-215 majority, driving trader consensus toward low implied probabilities of ouster by year-end deadlines. Persistent Freedom Caucus frustration over bipartisan spending deals, Ukraine aid, and government funding resolutions echoes the dynamics that toppled Kevin McCarthy, sustaining some removal risk. Recent passage of a short-term continuing resolution avoided a shutdown, but upcoming debt ceiling talks and potential omnibus bills could fracture the caucus. Traders weigh these tensions against post-election GOP discipline, with markets reflecting skin-in-the-game assessments of political catalysts.

question: Any US state legislature votes on secession by June 30, 2026?
description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if any house of any US state legislature holds a vote, as part of its standard legislative process, on a bill, resolution, or constitutional amendment whose main purpose is for the state to secede from the United States, or to otherwise declare its support for such a secession, by June 30, 2026 ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

A qualifying measure must clearly state its purpose as part of a process for the state to leave, withdraw from, dissolve political ties with, declare independence from, or otherwise terminate its membership in the United States; measures which merely study, explore, or explain a possibility of secession or independence will not qualify. Informal measures of consensus taking such as straw polls or whip counts will not qualify toward this market's resolution.

A vote on a resolution or other statement without binding legal effect but declaring the state's explicit support for secession will qualify toward this market's resolution.

Only a vote taken by a full chamber will qualify toward this market's resolution. Votes held by committees/subcommittees, etc., will not qualify.

This market's primary resolution source will be official records of qualifying votes from relevant state governments, however a consensus of credible reporting will also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus prices a 92% chance against any U.S. state legislature voting on secession by June 30, 2026, driven by firm constitutional barriers—established by the 1869 Supreme Court ruling in Texas v. White that unilateral secession is illegal without federal consent—and the absence of viable legislative momentum. Recent state sessions, including Texas where symbolic GOP resolutions surfaced in 2023 but failed to advance, show no active bills or votes on independence referendums; similar fringe efforts in Alaska and elsewhere remain stalled. Post-2024 election rhetoric has not translated to formal actions, underscoring traders' view of secession as politically untenable amid packed legislative agendas.

question: NATO x Russia military clash by March 31, 2026?
description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if there is a military encounter between the military forces of a NATO country and Russia between October 8, and March 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

A "military encounter" is defined as any incident involving the use of force such as missile strikes, artillery fire, exchange of gunfire, or other forms of direct military engagement between NATO and Russian military forces. Non-violent actions, such as airspace violations, firing of warning shots (such as the June, 2021 Black Sea Confrontations between Russian forces and HMS Defender), or cyberattacks will not qualify.

Interception of missiles or other one-way attack or loitering munitions (e.g. Shahed drones) which are targeting a 3rd party other than the listed countries or their respective forces will not alone qualify. Shooting down UAVs which are not munitions (e.g. MQ-9, Orlan 10, Orion, Bayraktar TB2, etc.) will qualify.

Intentional physical collisions, including aerial interceptions and naval ramming without the direct use of weaponry, such as the 2023 Black Sea incident—where a Russian Su-27 damaged a U.S. MQ-9 Reaper drone's propeller, leading to its crash— will not qualify regardless of damage.

Military contractors will qualify only if confirmed to be operating under the direct command or coordination of the respective state’s armed forces (e.g. the Battle of Khasham would not qualify).

The resolution source for this market will be a consensus of credible reporting.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on a NATO-Russia military clash reflects low implied probabilities, anchored by mutual deterrence amid the Ukraine conflict acting as a proxy war. Recent developments include Russian missiles briefly entering NATO airspace over Romania and Poland in late September 2024, prompting diplomatic rebukes but no armed response, and Ukraine's use of U.S.-supplied ATACMS missiles inside Russia, met with Putin's escalation warnings yet no direct NATO involvement. Putin has reiterated nuclear thresholds while NATO leaders, including Secretary General Rutte, emphasize de-escalation. The U.S. presidential election on November 5 could alter aid flows, potentially easing or heightening tensions, with traders watching post-election policy signals.

question: Will Rick Caruso win the California Governor Election in 2026?
description:
This market will resolve to according to the candidate who wins the 2026 California gubernatorial election currently scheduled for November 3, 2026.

If the results of the election are not confirmed by July 31, 2027, this market will resolve to "Other".

The resolution source for this market is the Associated Press, Fox News, and NBC. This market will resolve once all three sources call the race for the same candidate. If all three sources haven’t called the race in this state for the same candidate, this market will resolve based on official certification.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket strongly favors Democratic Congressman Eric Swalwell (61%) as the 2026 California gubernatorial winner, driven by the state's deep-blue electorate and his high-profile national presence amid Gov. Gavin Newsom's term limits creating an open primary. Supporting odds reflect a fragmented field, with Steve Hilton and Matt Mahan (both at 7.5%) gaining traction as moderate alternatives, while Riverside Sheriff Chad Bianco (5.9%) represents slim Republican hopes historically stifled in statewide races. Recent developments include Rep. Katie Porter's October 2024 exploratory committee launch for the race, yet her 2.3% odds signal trader skepticism post her Senate defeat; no major polling shifts or endorsements have altered Swalwell's lead, though early-stage liquidity tempers certainty ahead of formal announcements.

question: Will the Anaheim Ducks win the Pacific Division?
description:
This market will resolve according to the team that wins the listed division during the 2025-26 NHL regular season.

If at any point it becomes impossible for a listed team to win its division (e.g., mathematical elimination before season end), the relevant market will resolve immediately to “No.”

If the 2025-26 NHL season is permanently canceled or has not been completed by June 30, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to “Other.”

events.title: NHL: Pacific Division Winner
events.description:
This market will resolve according to the team that wins the listed division during the 2025-26 NHL regular season.

If at any point it becomes impossible for a listed team to win its division (e.g., mathematical elimination before season end), the relevant market will resolve immediately to “No.”

If the 2025-26 NHL season is permanently canceled or has not been completed by June 30, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to “Other.”

eventMetadata.context_description: Anaheim Ducks' dominant early-season surge, boasting the Pacific Division's best record through six games with key wins over divisional rivals, has propelled trader consensus to a 66.8% implied probability for them to claim the title. Recent developments bolstering this sentiment include Trevor Zegras' return from injury sparking offensive firepower and a stingy penalty kill ranking top-three league-wide, while challengers falter—Vegas Golden Knights (14.5%) nursing Eichel's day-to-day status after a flat start, and Edmonton Oilers (12.5%) hampered by defensive lapses despite McDavid's brilliance. Los Angeles Kings and others trail far behind amid roster inconsistencies and tougher schedules ahead, underscoring the Ducks' momentum edge in the wisdom-of-crowds pricing.

eventMetadata.context_description: Colorado Avalanche's dominant six-point lead atop the Central Division standings, fueled by Nathan MacKinnon's Hart Trophy-contending pace and a league-best offense averaging 3.7 goals per game, anchors their 84.3% implied probability as winners. Dallas Stars' 7.5% share reflects resilience despite Jason Robertson's injury absence, but defensive lapses loom large. Arizona Coyotes' surprising 4.1% nods to spoiler potential in a rebuild year, while Minnesota Wild's 1.4% fades amid goaltending woes and road-heavy slate.

eventMetadata.context_description: Carolina Hurricanes' commanding 12-point lead atop the Metropolitan Division standings with just four games remaining has traders pricing them at 95.1% to win the title, bolstered by their league-best 52 regulation wins and superior goal differential. Recent surges, including a five-game win streak fueled by Frederik Andersen's Vezina-caliber goaltending (2.22 GAA) and Sebastian Aho's 80-point season, have solidified their dominance after clinching a playoff spot. Rivals like the surging Penguins (3.1%) trail far behind at 90 points, hampered by injuries to Sidney Crosby sidelined briefly and a softer remaining schedule for Carolina. Realistic challenges include a freak injury outbreak or multi-game skid, but historical precedents show such lopsided leads rarely evaporate late.

eventMetadata.context_description: Buffalo Sabres command 50.3% trader consensus as Atlantic Division frontrunners, holding a narrow edge over Tampa Bay Lightning (44.0%) atop the standings after strong early-season surges, with Buffalo winning seven of their first 10 games behind Ukko-Pekka Luukkonen's goaltending and Tage Thompson's scoring outburst. Tampa counters with Nikita Kucherov's elite production and Andrei Vasilevskiy's proven playoff pedigree, splitting recent head-to-heads and boasting a rest advantage post-bye week. Minimal separation in points pace, power-play efficiency, and remaining schedule strength keeps odds bunched, as both clubs' momentum amid light injury reports underscores the division's volatility heading into holiday matchups.

If at any point it becomes impossible for a listed team to win the Western Conference (e.g., elimination from the playoffs), the relevant market will resolve immediately to “No.”

If the 2025-26 NHL season is permanently canceled or the playoffs have not been completed by August 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to “Other.”

eventMetadata.context_description: Colorado Avalanche command trader consensus at 33% implied probability for Western Conference champion, propelled by Nathan MacKinnon's league-leading scoring pace (1.50 points per game) and a recent five-game win streak that vaulted them to second in the Central Division behind Winnipeg. Dallas Stars (19.5%) follow closely, bolstered by Jake Oettinger's .915 save percentage and stingy defense allowing fewest goals in the West. Edmonton Oilers (12%) momentum builds with Connor McDavid and Leon Draisaitl heating up post-sluggish start, while Minnesota Wild (11.6%) ride a seven-game point streak and Vegas Golden Knights (11.5%) lean on playoff pedigree despite early inconsistencies and injuries to Mark Stone. Traders temper Winnipeg's league-best mark, citing regression risks in a grueling schedule.

question: Will Başakşehir win the Süper Lig?
description:
This market will resolve according to the listed team that is officially crowned the winner of the 2025–26 Turkish Süper Lig.

If at any point it becomes impossible for a listed team to win the Süper Lig title (e.g., mathematical elimination before the end of the season), the relevant market will resolve immediately to “No.”

If the 2025-26 Süper Lig season is permanently canceled or has not been completed by July 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to “Other.”

The primary resolution source will be official information from the Turkish Football Federation (https://www.tff.org/). A consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

events.title: Süper Lig Winner
events.description:
This market will resolve according to the listed team that is officially crowned the winner of the 2025–26 Turkish Süper Lig.

If at any point it becomes impossible for a listed team to win the Süper Lig title (e.g., mathematical elimination before the end of the season), the relevant market will resolve immediately to “No.”

If the 2025-26 Süper Lig season is permanently canceled or has not been completed by July 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to “Other.”

The primary resolution source will be official information from the Turkish Football Federation (https://www.tff.org/). A consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Galatasaray's commanding eight-point lead atop the Süper Lig standings after 13 matches, fueled by an unbeaten run and Victor Osimhen's prolific scoring form on loan, anchors their 75.5% trader consensus as champions. Fenerbahçe trails closely at 15.0% implied odds, buoyed by Jose Mourinho's tactical acumen and recent wins over rivals, though draws against weaker sides have dented momentum. Trabzonspor's 5.0% share reflects solid mid-table consistency and cup progress, while dark horses like Başakşehir and Göztepe (0.5% each) benefit from surprise surges, and Beşiktaş languishes at 0.1% amid defensive woes and managerial instability. Recent Galatasaray thrashings of Adana Demirspor and Fenerbahçe's Eyüpspor victory underscore shifting trader sentiment toward the leaders' title edge.

This market will resolve according to the listed team that is officially crowned the winner of the 2025–26 Turkish Süper Lig.

If at any point it becomes impossible for a listed team to win the Süper Lig title (e.g., mathematical elimination before the end of the season), the relevant market will resolve immediately to “No.”

If the 2025-26 Süper Lig season is permanently canceled or has not been completed by July 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to “Other.”

The primary resolution source will be official information from the Turkish Football Federation (https://www.tff.org/). A consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

events.title: Süper Lig Winner
events.description:
This market will resolve according to the listed team that is officially crowned the winner of the 2025–26 Turkish Süper Lig.

If at any point it becomes impossible for a listed team to win the Süper Lig title (e.g., mathematical elimination before the end of the season), the relevant market will resolve immediately to “No.”

If the 2025-26 Süper Lig season is permanently canceled or has not been completed by July 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to “Other.”

The primary resolution source will be official information from the Turkish Football Federation (https://www.tff.org/). A consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Galatasaray's commanding eight-point lead atop the Süper Lig standings after 13 matches, fueled by an unbeaten run and Victor Osimhen's prolific scoring form on loan, anchors their 75.5% trader consensus as champions. Fenerbahçe trails closely at 15.0% implied odds, buoyed by Jose Mourinho's tactical acumen and recent wins over rivals, though draws against weaker sides have dented momentum. Trabzonspor's 5.0% share reflects solid mid-table consistency and cup progress, while dark horses like Başakşehir and Göztepe (0.5% each) benefit from surprise surges, and Beşiktaş languishes at 0.1% amid defensive woes and managerial instability. Recent Galatasaray thrashings of Adana Demirspor and Fenerbahçe's Eyüpspor victory underscore shifting trader sentiment toward the leaders' title edge.

question: Will the Democrats win the Arizona governor race in 2026?
description:
This market will resolve according to the winner of the 2026 Arizona gubernatorial election.

A candidate shall be considered to represent a party in the event that he or she is the nominee of the party in question. Candidates other than the Democratic or Republican nominee (e.g., Greens, Libertarian, independent) may be added at a later date. 

Candidates who run as independents will not be encompassed by the “Democrat” or “Republican” options regardless of any affiliation they may have with the party. 

The resolution source for this market is the Associated Press, Fox News, and NBC. This market will resolve once all three sources call the race for the same candidate. If all three sources haven’t called the race in this state for the same candidate, this market will resolve based on the official certification.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus strongly favors Democrat Katie Hobbs retaining the Arizona governorship, with 70.5% implied probability reflecting her incumbency advantage after a narrow 2022 victory over Kari Lake. Recent polling averages, such as those from Data for Progress and Quantus Insights, show Hobbs leading hypothetical Republican matchups by 10-15 points amid Arizona's purple-state dynamics. The Republican field remains unsettled, lacking a clear frontrunner despite names like Karrin Taylor Robson floating, hampered by party infighting post-2024 election setbacks. Hobbs' approval hovers around 50%, buoyed by handling of water policy and border security initiatives, while upcoming 2026 primaries could shift dynamics but currently reinforce Democratic edge.

question: Will any country leave NATO by June 30, 2026?
description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if any member state formally withdraws from NATO or provides an official notice of denunciation to NATO by June 30, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

A notice of denunciation refers to the submission of a notice of withdrawal as per Article 13 of the North Atlantic Treaty.

A country's exit from NATO’s integrated military command structure will not be sufficient to resolve this market to "Yes". That country must either withdraw or submit a notice of denunciation to trigger a "Yes" resolution.

The resolution source will be official information from the relevant government and NATO, however a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

events.title: Will any country leave NATO by...?
events.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if any member state formally withdraws from NATO or provides an official notice of denunciation to NATO by December 31, 2025, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

A notice of denunciation refers to the submission of a notice of withdrawal as per Article 13 of the North Atlantic Treaty.

A country's exit from NATO’s integrated military command structure will not be sufficient to resolve this market to "Yes". That country must either withdraw or submit a notice of denunciation to trigger a "Yes" resolution.

The resolution source will be official information from the relevant government and NATO, however a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: NATO's unprecedented unity amid Russia's war in Ukraine remains the dominant factor suppressing trader odds for any member departure, with all 32 allies recommitting at the July 2024 Washington summit to collective defense and boosted spending targets. No country has invoked Article 13's one-year exit clause, and recent expansions via Finland and Sweden underscore alliance resilience over fragmentation. Skepticism from leaders like Hungary's Orbán or past French autonomy rhetoric has not escalated to withdrawal threats. Upcoming U.S. election results could spark funding debates, but historical precedents show no full exits since 1949, reflecting trader consensus on sustained cohesion.

eventMetadata.context_description: Alabama's entrenched Republican dominance, evidenced by consistent double-digit presidential margins and GOP supermajorities in the legislature, underpins the 94.2% trader consensus for a Republican gubernatorial winner in 2026. Lt. Gov. Will Ainsworth holds a commanding lead in early GOP primary polls at around 40%, bolstered by his statewide experience, strong fundraising, and alignment with Trump-era conservatism, following Gov. Kay Ivey's term limit. Democrats trail far behind with fragmented fields and minimal viability in this deep-red state. Realistic challenges include a major Ainsworth scandal or GOP primary implosion consolidating behind a weaker nominee, though historical precedents suggest low odds of a Democratic upset absent a national blue wave.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket prices a Republican victory in the Arkansas governor race at 93%, reflecting the state's entrenched GOP dominance—evident in the party's sweep of recent statewide contests, including incumbent Sarah Huckabee Sanders' 2022 landslide win by 29 points—and her sustained high approval ratings above 55%. With no major Democratic candidates announced and Arkansas's R+15 partisan lean, the Republican nominee holds a commanding edge absent surprises. Realistic challenges include Sanders unexpectedly retiring, a damaging scandal emerging, or a national Democratic wave boosting turnout, though historical base rates in deep-red states like Arkansas show such upsets rare, with GOP governors winning by double digits in the last four cycles. Primaries in 2026 could clarify fields and shift odds.

eventMetadata.context_description: Colorado's gubernatorial race favors Democrats at 93% trader consensus, driven by the party's structural advantages including a voter registration edge of over 100,000 and landslide wins in recent statewide contests like Gov. Jared Polis's 19-point 2022 victory. U.S. Rep. Brittany Pettersen's August announcement as the Democratic frontrunner has solidified her lead, with early polls showing her topping hypothetical GOP matchups by 15+ points and strong initial fundraising. Republicans lack a clear heavyweight contender post-2022 nominee Heidi Ganahl's defeat. Realistic challenges include a unified GOP behind a high-profile recruit, a Democratic scandal, or a national midterm wave shifting voter sentiment amid economic pressures.

eventMetadata.context_description: **Connecticut's enduring Democratic dominance** anchors trader sentiment, pricing Democrats at 93% to win the 2026 gubernatorial race amid a nearly 2-to-1 voter registration advantage over Republicans. The state hasn't elected a GOP governor since Jodi Rell left office in 2011, with recent polling aggregates showing frontrunners like Lt. Gov. Susan Bysiewicz or AG William Tong leading primary rivals and general election opponents by 15-25 points. Strong Democratic fundraising, Lamont administration approval ratings above 50%, and urban/suburban strongholds sustain this edge. Upsets remain possible via Democratic nominee controversy, GOP unity behind a high-profile challenger like Bob Stefanowski, economic shocks, or a 2024 national Republican landslide spillover, though traders assign low odds without fresh catalysts. Primaries in summer 2026 could shift dynamics.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus heavily favors a Democratic victory in the Hawaii gubernatorial election, pricing Democrats at 93.5% implied probability amid the state's entrenched partisan history—no Republican has won since 1959—and strong polling for the party's frontrunners. Incumbent Governor Josh Green, seeking re-election, leads early surveys by wide margins in both the August 2026 primary and November general, bolstered by Hawaii's consistent 60-70% Democratic presidential margins and robust fundraising. Recent catalysts include Green's announcement and favorable economic indicators like tourism recovery. Realistic challenges include a damaging scandal, primary chaos fracturing the Democratic vote, or a standout Republican like former lawmaker Andria Tupola surging via national GOP support, though historical base rates suggest low upset risk ahead of key filing deadlines.

eventMetadata.context_description: Idaho's entrenched Republican dominance, with over 50% voter registration advantage and consistent 20-30 point gubernatorial margins, anchors the 94.7% trader consensus for a GOP win in the 2026 election. Incumbent Brad Little, eligible for a third term after his 60% 2022 reelection amid a tight primary, remains the presumed frontrunner absent announcement otherwise, while Democrats field no high-profile challengers and trail in early generic ballot polls by wide margins. This reflects structural red-state dynamics reinforced by 2024's Trump landslide. Realistic disruptions include a divisive GOP primary producing a weak nominee, major scandal, or unprecedented Democratic turnout surge, though base rates favor continuity ahead of May 2026 primaries.

eventMetadata.context_description: Polls consistently show Iowa's 2026 open-seat gubernatorial race deadlocked between Democrat Rob Sand, the popular state auditor known for bipartisan audits, and Republican Mike Nunn, a veteran and businessman pitching economic growth. Trader consensus reflects this 47.5-47.5 split, driven by Iowa's GOP lean—evident in recent presidential margins—but offset by Reynolds' term limits and Sand's cross-aisle appeal amid voter frustration over property taxes. Recent Emerson and PPIC surveys confirm the tie within margins of error, with low name recognition keeping dynamics fluid. Separation could emerge from June 2026 primaries, Trump-endorsed picks, fundraising surges, or debates highlighting policy contrasts on agriculture and education.

A candidate shall be considered to represent a party in the event that he or she is the nominee of the party in question. Candidates other than the Democratic or Republican nominee (e.g., Greens, Libertarian, independent) may be added at a later date.

Candidates who run as independents will not be encompassed by the “Democrat” or “Republican” options regardless of any affiliation they may have with the party.

The resolution source for this market is the Associated Press, Fox News, and NBC. This market will resolve once all three sources call the race for the same candidate. If all three sources haven’t called the race in this state for the same candidate, this market will resolve based on the official certification.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus prices Democrats at 90% to win the 2026 Maine gubernatorial election, reflecting early polling advantages for state House Majority Leader Laurel Libby over former U.S. Rep. Bruce Poliquin. Recent Suffolk University/Globe (October 2024) and Emerson College polls show Libby leading 37-42% to Poliquin's 32-34%, with undecideds at 18-24%, underscoring Maine's D+2 partisan lean and Democratic dominance in recent statewide races despite Gov. Janet Mills' term limits creating an open seat. Absent major GOP surges, traders weigh the incumbent party's organizational edge and historical base rates favoring Democrats in the Pine Tree State.

eventMetadata.context_description: Incumbent Democratic Governor Wes Moore holds a commanding lead in trader consensus for Maryland's 2026 gubernatorial race, reflecting the state's deep-blue partisan makeup where Democrats outregister Republicans 2-to-1 and have won every contest since 1986. Moore's 2022 victory margin exceeded 30 points, bolstered by strong approval ratings above 55% amid effective budget management and economic growth initiatives. Recent fundraising dominance, with Moore raising over $5 million early, underscores frontrunner momentum amid a fragmented Republican field lacking a clear heavyweight. Realistic challenges include a major scandal eroding Moore's popularity, a national Republican wave, or an upset GOP nominee consolidating conservative turnout, though historical base rates in Maryland suggest low upset probability.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus assigns a 93.6% implied probability to a Democratic win in the Massachusetts gubernatorial election, anchored by the state's entrenched Democratic dominance—supermajority legislative control, 37% Democratic voter registration versus 9% Republican, and consistent generic ballot leads exceeding 25 points in recent polls. Incumbent Governor Maura Healey's strong approval ratings above 60% and her 2022 landslide victory reinforce this frontrunner status, with no major challengers yet emerging. Realistic disruptions include Healey opting out for a national bid, exposing a divided Democratic primary, a high-profile GOP fundraiser like Chris Doughty consolidating support, or unforeseen economic shocks eroding incumbent advantages, though Massachusetts' partisan history limits Republican paths to victory.

eventMetadata.context_description: Gretchen Whitmer's term limit creates an open Michigan gubernatorial race in 2026, with trader consensus favoring Democrats at 64.5% due to the party's hold on statewide offices and early polling advantages in this battleground state. Recent surveys, including a Glengariff Group poll showing Democratic prospects like Lt. Gov. Garlin Gilchrist leading hypothetical Republican matchups by 8-12 points, bolster this sentiment amid stable voter registration edges for Democrats. Republicans at 22.5% reflect challenges post-2024, with figures like Tudor Dixon or Mike Rogers lacking a clear frontrunner despite fundraising starts. Upcoming candidate filings and 2025 legislative sessions could shift dynamics, but current wisdom-of-crowds pricing emphasizes Democratic structural strengths over GOP enthusiasm.

eventMetadata.context_description: Minnesota's Democratic gubernatorial dominance, marked by Tim Walz's 2022 reelection victory and the party's trifecta control of state government, anchors the 92.5% trader consensus for a Democratic winner in the 2026 race. Recent polling shows Walz retaining strong approval despite his vice presidential bid, with a deep Democratic bench including Lt. Gov. Peggy Flanagan and deep-pocketed donors bolstering the frontrunner position amid limited Republican announcements beyond figures like Scott Jensen. Trader odds reflect historical base rates favoring Democrats in population-rich metro areas. Realistic challenges include a high-profile GOP recruit amid a post-2024 national Republican surge, Walz's potential VP success creating an open seat, or unexpected scandals eroding Democratic turnout. Key catalysts ahead: candidate filings and 2024 election fallout.

eventMetadata.context_description: Republican dominance drives the 92% trader consensus for a GOP victory in Nebraska's 2026 gubernatorial race, rooted in the state's reliable conservative electorate and historical precedents—no Democrat has won since Ben Nelson's 1990 triumph. Incumbent Governor Jim Pillen, eligible for re-election after his 2022 26-point win, leads early polls by wide margins amid lopsided Republican voter registration edges (over 50% GOP vs. 25% Dem). Recent primary filings and fundraising reports reinforce this frontrunner positioning, with minimal Democratic challengers emerging. Upsets would hinge on rare catalysts like a GOP nominee scandal, economic shocks eroding rural support, or an improbable national blue wave, though current evidence points to stability ahead of spring 2026 primaries.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket tilts slightly toward Democrats at 52.5% for Nevada's 2026 gubernatorial race versus Republicans at 46%, mirroring the state's purple battleground status with elections often hinging on margins under 3%. Incumbent Gov. Joe Lombardo (R) benefits from re-election name recognition amid steady approval ratings around 50%, but Democratic contenders like Attorney General Aaron Ford and Secretary of State Cisco Aguilar have surged in early fundraising and internal polls showing ties within 2 points. Independent voters and Las Vegas turnout dynamics keep it razor-tight; catalysts for separation include June 2026 primaries, economic data on tourism and housing, or shifting national political winds.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on the New Hampshire gubernatorial race strongly favors the Republican nominee at 72.5%, driven by consistent double-digit polling leads for Kelly Ayotte over Democrat Joyce Craig in recent surveys from Emerson College (49%-37%) and American Research Group (52%-37%). Traders price these risks low, reflecting the wisdom of crowds.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus slightly favors Democrats at 51% in the Ohio gubernatorial race, driven by the state's battleground status and fragmented early fields lacking dominant frontrunners. Republican Secretary of State Frank LaRose leads GOP polls at around 25%, trailed by Attorney General Dave Yost, while Democrats see former Health Director Amy Acton and ex-Dayton Mayor Nan Whaley in a tight pack under 20% each. Trump's 2024 Ohio landslide bolsters Republican base turnout prospects, but Democratic edges on abortion rights—bolstered by 2023's Issue 1 victory—and moderate voter appeal keep it neck-and-neck amid DeWine's term limits. Separation could emerge from national endorsements, 2026 primaries, fundraising surges, or economic shifts influencing swing voters.

eventMetadata.context_description: Oklahoma's deeply Republican political landscape drives the 93% trader consensus for a GOP gubernatorial victory in 2026, reflecting the state's consistent election of Republican governors since 2010, GOP supermajorities in the legislature, and a 33-point Trump margin in 2020. Republican Attorney General Gentner Drummond's July announcement as a leading candidate, bolstered by his high approval ratings amid high-profile actions against state officials, has solidified frontrunner status without a strong Democratic contender emerging. Voter registration favors Republicans 2-to-1, and early polling shows GOP candidates leading by wide margins. Realistic challenges include a divisive GOP primary eroding unity, a major scandal hitting the nominee, or an unforeseen Democratic recruitment surge, though historical base rates suggest low upset probability.

eventMetadata.context_description: Incumbent Democrat Michael Bennet's double-digit leads in recent statewide polls against Republican Jeff Hurd underpin the 92% trader consensus for a Democratic victory in Colorado's Senate race. Bennet benefits from strong fundraising, incumbency advantage, and the state's left-leaning tilt, where Democrats have won the last four Senate contests by wide margins amid a Democratic governor and legislature. Hurd, a moderate western slope congressman hopeful, trails despite GOP national headwinds, with no major catalysts shifting momentum. Realistic challenges include a dramatic Republican turnout surge, Bennet scandal, or national red wave overwhelming Colorado's blue base rates, though time constraints limit upset potential ahead of November.

eventMetadata.context_description: Lisa Blunt Rochester's commanding lead in recent polls, averaging 20-30 points over Republican Eric Ram, anchors trader consensus at 93.5% for a Democratic hold in Delaware's Senate race. The state's deep blue tilt—evident in Biden's 19-point 2020 win and unbroken Democratic Senate control since 1973—bolsters this pricing, alongside Blunt Rochester's fundraising dominance and congressional experience. GOP historical base rates show no competitive statewide wins in decades. Realistic challengers include a major Democratic scandal, unexpected national Republican surge, or late polling shift, though none have materialized amid stable voter registration edges for Democrats; watch October debates for volatility.
